$comment: MCM Punitive Articles retrieval benchmark. Each entry has a natural-language query and gold labels for evaluation. gold.article_numbers are sufficient for basic recall; gold.chunk_ids provide fine-grained evaluation once the index is built and specific chunk IDs are inspected. CHANGES v1.1: fixed total_questions (was 3, now 10); normalised article_numbers for 112a; added difficulty-weighted notes.

metadata.notes: Gold chunk_ids are intentionally empty until first index run. After building the index, run with --verbose, inspect which chunk_ids contain the actual answer content, and populate chunk_ids for fine-grained Precision evaluation.

questions.query: What does Article 92 cover and what are its elements?

gold.notes: Article 92 covers failure to obey order or regulation. Three offenses: (1) violation of lawful general order/regulation, (2) failure to obey other lawful order, (3) dereliction of duty. Relevant chunks should contain the 'b. Elements' section.

questions.query: What are the elements of Article 86 unauthorized absence?

gold.notes: Article 86 AWOL/UA. Elements: accused was required to be at a certain place at a certain time, accused knew of the requirement, accused failed to appear or departed without authority.

questions.query: What is the maximum punishment for larceny under Article 121?

gold.notes: Article 121 larceny and wrongful appropriation. Maximum punishment depends on value of property and type (larceny vs wrongful appropriation). Relevant chunks must contain the 'd. Maximum punishment' section.

questions.query: What constitutes assault under the UCMJ?

gold.notes: Article 128 assault. 2024 MCM has three categories: simple assault, assault consummated by battery, aggravated assault. This query has no article number — tests semantic retrieval quality.

questions.query: What are the elements of insubordinate conduct toward a warrant officer?

gold.notes: Article 91 insubordinate conduct toward warrant officer, noncommissioned officer, or petty officer. Hard because 'warrant officer' is not in the article title; tests whether the system retrieves Art. 91 over Art. 89/90.

questions.query: What does Article 134 cover as a general article?

gold.notes: Article 134 general article covers disorders/neglects prejudicial to good order and discipline, conduct of a nature to bring discredit upon the armed forces, and crimes/offenses not capital.

questions.query: How does the MCM define conspiracy under Article 81?

gold.notes: Article 81 conspiracy: agreement between two or more persons to commit a UCMJ offense, plus an overt act by one of the conspirators in furtherance of the agreement.

questions.query: What is dereliction of duty and how is it proven?

gold.notes: Dereliction of duty is charged under Article 92(3). Elements: accused had prescribed duties, accused knew or should have known of those duties, accused was willfully or negligently derelict. Note: same article as q001 but targets a specific sub-offense.

questions.query: What offenses fall under conduct unbecoming an officer?

gold.notes: Article 133 conduct unbecoming an officer and a gentleman. Hard because 'conduct unbecoming' is a broad concept; system must retrieve Art. 133 not Art. 134.

questions.query: What is the punishment for wrongful use of controlled substances?

gold.notes: Article 112a wrongful use, possession, manufacture, distribution of controlled substances. Note: article number contains 'a' suffix — tests normalisation of sub-article numbers in is_relevant().
